expectations.text: Uses t() for all user-facing strings
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 2 Translations: 'All user-visible strings MUST use t()'. Rule is explicit and unconditional. Code example shows t('openregister', 'Objects') and t('openregister', 'Add new item') in the template.

SKILL.md Step 2: 'First argument is always the app name (e.g., openregister)'. The t() function signature and app name convention are specified. Code examples consistently show t(appName, translationKey) pattern.

SKILL.md does not explicitly state 'default to English'. The Dutch Government Frontend Standards reference (references/dutch-gov-frontend-standards.md) covers multi-language support but is a reference file, not inlined. SKILL.md only says 'all strings via t()' — the English-default rule is not explicitly in the main SKILL.md instructions.

expectations.text: Follows NL Design System CSS variables
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md CSS & Styling Rules: 'Support NL Design System tokens (nldesign app)', 'Use Nextcloud CSS variables: var(--color-main-text), var(--color-primary), etc.', 'never hardcode colors'. Standards Quick Reference: 'Accessibility: WCAG AA required'.

notes: t() usage, app-name first argument, and NL Design System CSS variables are explicitly specified. Gap: 'defaults to English' is in the reference file (dutch-gov-frontend-standards.md) but not in the SKILL.md main instructions. Adding 'English is the base language — translation files use English keys and English values' to the Translations section would close this.
